definition: Inflation expectations remain anchored but the buffer is thinner than at any point since the late 1990s. 5Y breakevens at ~2.3-2.5% remain within the 'anchored' band (1.8-2.5%), and the Coibion-Gorodnichenko threshold for de-anchoring (sustained 5Y breakeven divergence >75-100bp for >6 months) has not been breached. However, the distribution has shifted rightward toward the upper bound.
evidence: 5Y breakevens at ~2.3-2.5%, within historical anchored range (1.8-2.5%)
Michigan 5-10Y consumer expectations at 3.0-3.2% — elevated but not de-anchored
5Y breakevens peaked at ~3.0-3.1% briefly in early 2022, settled back to 2.3-2.5%
Fed hiked 525bp and inflation dropped from 5.6% to ~2.5-2.8% without recession — transmission working with a lag
open_questions: Will tariff-driven goods inflation push realized inflation above 3.5% for 2-3 consecutive quarters, triggering Phillips Curve shift from 'anchored, flat' to 'de-anchoring, steepening'?
At what point does the rightward distribution shift breach the anchoring threshold?
Is the Coibion-Gorodnichenko threshold itself regime-dependent?
